title: Worker Boot & CLI Empty Response -- 6 Blocking Issues

root_causes.fix: Set isolationOption.enabled=true in user + workspace settings

root_causes.issue: SendKeys(ENTER) fails in Chromium render widgets
root_causes.fix: keybd_event(0x0D) hardware-level Enter via Win32 API

root_causes.issue: 20px UIA Edit accessibility placeholder wins Edit scoring, ghost-type pastes to wrong element
root_causes.fix: Name filter skips not accessible|screen reader Edit controls

root_causes.issue: UIA ExpandCollapsePattern opens dropdown but loses window focus; keyboard goes to wrong window
root_causes.fix: pyautogui.click() on button coords (opens AND focuses), typewrite for Chromium content

root_causes.issue: Permissions guard typewrite(bypass) leaks into chat if Chromium dropdown renders slowly
root_causes.fix: Escape pre-clear, 1.5s render delay, Ctrl+A+Backspace cleanup on retry

root_causes.issue: pyautogui.click() for dropdown opens steals mouse cursor
root_causes.fix: UIA InvokePattern for opens (no mouse movement), pyautogui keyboard-only for Chromium content

architecture_knowledge.chromium_quickpick: VS Code quickpick overlays are Chromium-rendered, invisible to UIA/Win32/SendKeys. Only hardware-level input works.
architecture_knowledge.ghost_type_pipeline: Chat input is Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND not UIA Edit. Delivery: clipboard -> focus render -> Ctrl+V -> keybd_event Enter
architecture_knowledge.edit_scoring_trap: 20px accessibility placeholder Edit passes height/width filters. Must filter by name.
architecture_knowledge.focus_loss: UIA ExpandCollapsePattern opens dropdown but loses focus. pyautogui.click() both opens and focuses.

boot_prerequisites: isolationOption.enabled: true in user AND workspace settings
ghost-type uses keybd_event for Enter (not SendKeys)
Permissions guard has Escape pre-clear and text cleanup on retry
Edit scoring filters accessibility placeholder by name
Workers opened ONE AT A TIME with full UIA verification
No pyautogui.click() for dropdown opens -- UIA InvokePattern only

title: CLI Isolation Setting Drift Blocks All Delegation

root_causes.issue: github.copilot.chat.cli.isolationOption.enabled silently changed from false to true in user settings between sessions
root_causes.fix: Set isolationOption.enabled back to false in user settings
root_causes.commit: Added tools/skynet_isolation_guard.py + boot guards in all 3 Start scripts

impact: ALL CLI delegation/dispatch to workers silently cancelled. Workers never receive tasks. No error message shown -- delegation just does not happen.
prevention: Isolation guard runs at boot time in all 3 start scripts
tools/skynet_isolation_guard.py checks both user and workspace settings
Auto-fixes user setting if it drifts to true
Workspace setting has explicit override to false
Can run --watch mode for continuous monitoring
lesson: VS Code user settings can change between sessions (auto-updates, sync, experiments). All critical settings must be verified at boot time, not assumed stable.

title: HardwareEnter Focus Theft -- Stuck Unsubmitted Prompt in Beta
symptom: V8 paper revision task text visible in Beta input box but NEVER SUBMITTED. dispatch_log shows success=True result_received=False.

root_causes.issue: keybd_event(0x0D) HardwareEnter sends VK_RETURN to OS input queue, hitting whatever has keyboard focus -- NOT targeted to specific HWND. Apply dialog (30 files changed +7563 -129) at top of Beta window stole Chromium internal focus during 300ms gap between Ctrl+V paste and Enter.
root_causes.code_location: tools/skynet_dispatch.py lines 999-1003 (HardwareEnter), lines 1183/1205/1232/1255 (300ms Sleep before Enter)
root_causes.mechanism: Paste path: SendKeys.SendWait(^v) -> Sleep 300ms -> keybd_event(VK_RETURN). During 300ms gap, VS Code Apply dialog captures Chromium keyboard focus. keybd_event Enter goes to Apply area instead of chat input. Text sits in input box unsubmitted.
root_causes.fix_proposed: Replace keybd_event with PostMessage WM_KEYDOWN/WM_CHAR directly to renderHwnd (HWND-targeted, immune to focus theft). Alternative: reduce 300ms gap + add post-Enter verification.

root_causes.issue: IDLE->IDLE false positive in _verify_delivery() masks the failure. Lines 1877-1879 assume IDLE->IDLE means CLI processed fast. In reality it means Enter never reached chat input.
root_causes.mechanism: Worker stays IDLE (no task submitted). After 8s polling, _verify_delivery sees pre_state==IDLE and final_state==IDLE, returns True with log ASSUMED OK. This prevents retry logic (lines 1766-1798) from ever firing.
root_causes.fix_proposed: Remove IDLE->IDLE assumption. Return False when state unchanged after 8s. Let retry logic at lines 1766-1798 re-attempt delivery.

cascade_analysis.ghost_type_to_worker: Returns True -- PS script reports OK_RENDER_ATTACHED because paste succeeded. PS has no way to verify Enter reached correct target.
cascade_analysis._execute_ghost_dispatch: Returns True -- stdout contains OK_*, returncode==0.
cascade_analysis._verify_delivery: Returns True -- IDLE->IDLE false positive at line 1877.
cascade_analysis.dispatch_to_worker: Returns True -- both ghost_type and verify report success.
cascade_analysis.dispatch_log: Records success=True, result_received=False. No alarm raised.
cascade_analysis.net_effect: Silent total delivery failure with no detection at any layer.

proposed_fixes.title: PostMessage WM_KEYDOWN to render widget HWND
proposed_fixes.description: Replace keybd_event(0x0D) with PostMessage(renderHwnd, WM_KEYDOWN, VK_RETURN, 0) + PostMessage(renderHwnd, WM_KEYUP, VK_RETURN, 0). HWND-targeted Enter is immune to focus theft. Fallback: PostMessage(renderHwnd, WM_CHAR, 0x000D, 0) if WM_KEYDOWN is ignored by Chromium.
proposed_fixes.risk: MEDIUM -- Chromium may not process WM_KEYDOWN via PostMessage for all key codes. Needs testing. WM_CHAR with carriage return is more reliable for text input.

proposed_fixes.title: Remove IDLE->IDLE false positive in _verify_delivery()
proposed_fixes.description: Lines 1877-1879: change return True to return False. IDLE->IDLE after 8s polling means delivery was NOT confirmed. This unblocks the retry logic at lines 1766-1798 which will re-attempt ghost_type + Enter.
proposed_fixes.risk: LOW -- may cause extra retries for genuinely fast-processed messages, but that is preferable to silent delivery failure.

proposed_fixes.title: Post-Enter input text consumption check
proposed_fixes.description: After HardwareEnter(), wait 500ms and check if render widget input still contains dispatch text (via UIA or clipboard re-read of input). If text remains, Enter was not received by chat input -- fire pyautogui.press(enter) as targeted fallback.
proposed_fixes.risk: LOW -- adds 500ms latency per dispatch. UIA access to input content may be unreliable.

related_incidents: INCIDENT_014 (SendKeys ENTER fails on Chromium render widgets)

architecture_knowledge.keybd_event_is_untargeted: keybd_event(VK_RETURN) sends through OS input queue to current focus owner. NOT HWND-targeted. Any focus change between paste and Enter causes misdelivery.
architecture_knowledge.300ms_gap_is_vulnerability_window: The 300ms Sleep between Ctrl+V and HardwareEnter exists to let Chromium process the paste. But any dialog/overlay/internal focus shift during this window causes Enter to go to wrong target.
architecture_knowledge.apply_dialog_steals_focus: VS Code Apply dialog (file diff review) is rendered at top of Copilot CLI window. It competes with chat input for Chromium internal keyboard focus. Particularly dangerous during paste operations.
architecture_knowledge.idle_idle_is_ambiguous: IDLE->IDLE after polling means EITHER (a) message submitted and processed fast OR (b) Enter never reached chat input. Cannot distinguish without checking input text state.

resolution_note: FIX_B (IDLE->IDLE false positive) applied at skynet_dispatch.py L2147-2151,L2177-2180 as part of INCIDENT_018 fix. Retry logic at L1969-1993 handles unverified delivery correctly. PostMessageEnter (FIX_A) also applied for CHROME_RENDER paths. Closed 2026-03-23.

title: Apply Panel Blocks Ghost-Type Enter Key Submission
root_cause: When workers generate code changes in Copilot CLI mode, VS Code creates an Apply panel (showing N files changed). This Apply panel steals internal Chromium focus away from the chat input box. The ghost_type dispatch pipeline writes text to the chat input via clipboard paste (PostMessage WM_PASTE), which succeeds. However, the subsequent Enter key submission via keybd_event(0x0D) sends VK_RETURN to the OS input queue, which delivers it to whatever has focus -- the Apply panel, not the chat input. Result: prompt text appears in the input box but is never submitted. The worker appears IDLE with unsubmitted text.
symptoms: Worker shows IDLE state but has unsubmitted text in chat input
Apply panel visible in worker window with N files changed banner

fix_applied.description: Three-layer fix by Beta+Alpha, cross-validated by Delta: (1) SafeEnter: checks GetForegroundWindow before and after Enter, restores focus if stolen. EDIT path now passes child Edit HWND instead of top-level window HWND. (2) PostMessageEnter: for CHROME_RENDER paths, sends WM_KEYDOWN/WM_KEYUP VK_RETURN directly to the render widget HWND via PostMessage, bypassing the OS input queue entirely. (3) _verify_delivery false positives removed: IDLE->IDLE no longer returns True with ASSUMED_OK -- now returns False with WARN UNVERIFIED, preventing dispatch_log from recording success=True on unsubmitted prompts.

recovery_procedure: If an Apply panel is blocking a worker: (1) press Escape to dismiss the panel, (2) type /clear + Enter to clear session, (3) re-dispatch via ghost_type. This is keyboard-only -- no cursor movement. SetForegroundWindow ensures the correct window has focus before keyboard input.
prevention: Workers MUST use Copilot CLI mode (not Agent/Edits) per INCIDENT 015
PostMessageEnter bypasses OS input queue for CHROME_RENDER paths
SafeEnter verifies focus before and after for EDIT paths
lessons_learned: keybd_event sends to OS input queue (whatever has focus), not to a target HWND
PostMessage WM_KEYDOWN/WM_KEYUP is the safe alternative for targeted key delivery
Apply panels in Copilot CLI steal Chromium focus silently
_verify_delivery must never assume IDLE->IDLE means success

title: CDP Extension Install Non-Functional - Session-Only Flag and Quoting Bugs
root_cause: 4 bugs in Gamma CDP extension install (cdp.py, agent.py, cdp_install_all.py): (1) --load-extension is session-only -- Chrome loads unpacked extensions for that launch session only, they do NOT persist to the Extensions directory and vanish on restart. (2) --profile-directory quoting bug in all 3 files -- f-string embeds literal quote chars inside subprocess list arg (--profile-directory='"Profile 17"') making Chrome match no real profile. (3) --load-extension flag is IGNORED when Chrome is already running -- Chrome just opens a new tab in the existing instance without applying the flag. (4) agent.py install_extension() uses get_user_data_dir() (real Chrome dir) but extension is session-only anyway.
what_failed: Gamma added --load-extension flag to 3 files (cdp.py launch(), agent.py install_extension(), cdp_install_all.py launch_chrome()) but the Chrome Bridge extension never appeared in chrome://extensions on the SOCIALS profile (Profile 17). The extension was never persistently installed.
fix: PARTIAL: Fixed BUG 2 (quoting bug) in all 3 files -- removed inner quotes from --profile-directory args. BUG 1 (session-only) requires a fundamentally different approach: either (a) use chrome.management API for persistent install, (b) manually copy extension to profile Extensions dir with Preferences entry, or (c) always include --load-extension on every Chrome CDP launch (cdp.py launch() already does this for the sandbox dir). BUG 3 (Chrome already running) requires killing Chrome before relaunching with the flag.
lesson: Cross-validation MUST include functional runtime testing, not just py_compile syntax checks. Alpha cross-validated Gamma with verdict PASS_WITH_BUG but never tested if the extension actually loaded in Chrome. Syntax verification catches ~10% of real bugs. For external system integration tasks (Chrome, databases, APIs), cross-validation must verify the actual integration works end-to-end.

missed_bugs: BUG 2: --profile-directory quoting bug embeds literal quotes in all 3 files
BUG 3: --load-extension ignored when Chrome already running
BUG 4: agent.py uses incompatible real user data dir
cross_validation_failure.what_alpha_checked: py_compile syntax, code logic presence of --load-extension flag
cross_validation_failure.what_alpha_missed: Actual Chrome extension loading, persistence across restarts, behavior with running Chrome, subprocess quoting semantics
